subject: Replace exFAT with version supporting symlinks.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.8 (Class G) discusses the v5.15 equivalents (8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27)

impact.effect_if_absent: Symlinks on /media/fat (the exFAT data partition) are not readable. MiSTer's arcade organizer builds its entire _Organized tree via symlinks; removing this commit without a replacement silently breaks the organizer. This is a feature loss, not a loud build error.

userspace_coupling.interface: exfat filesystem symlink support (readlink, mkdir/create operations on symlinks)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer file_io.cpp:1592 relies on symlinks in _Organized arcade filesystem

forward_port.conflicts: No direct forward-port needed; 6.18 uses vanilla mainline exfat + our 0031 patch (0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch) for Samsung-compatible symlink support per ADR 0010 + ADR 0019

superseded_by: df35bdb27 (Add exFAT with symlinks support, v5.15)

license_provenance.author: Samsung; re-integrated by Sorgelig into Linux-Kernel_MiSTer

notes:
This is the v5.13.12-branch precursor to the v5.15 split commits (8b6b8c2f5 'Remove original exFAT driver' + df35bdb27 'Add exFAT with symlinks support'). It is NOT part of the stock v5.15 kernel or our current 6.18 build. All the work here—replacing mainline exfat with Samsung's out-of-tree driver—was done in a single large all-in-one commit on the old v5.13.12 branch (2021-08-25), then reorganized in v5.15 (2021-08-30) as two separate commits for cleaner history. In 6.18, we have DROPPED the out-of-tree driver entirely (per ADR 0010) and instead use vanilla mainline exfat + our carried patch 0031 (0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, per ADR 0019 option (c)) to restore Samsung-compatible symlink format support. The symlink feature is still available to MiSTer; dropping this old driver did not regress the user experience. This record is filed for historical traceability only; no action is needed for this commit itself.

sonnet-verified: CONFIRMED, chain of references fully re-derived and holds, no amendments needed. (1) `git show --stat ffbb77e46e` in /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer matches exactly: 35 files, +12135/-7412. (2) Branch topology re-derived independently via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`: ffbb77e46e IS an ancestor of origin/MiSTer-v5.13.12 and is NOT an ancestor of origin/MiSTer-v5.15; conversely 8b6b8c2f538f1f4a066b2a99bead4a2ddb3fb07a and df35bdb271325891b3a03c75097541d5b76fbeb6 ARE ancestors of origin/MiSTer-v5.15 and are NOT ancestors of origin/MiSTer-v5.13.12 — confirms the 'v5.13.12 all-in-one precursor, split apart on v5.15' framing exactly, and both split-commit shas resolve to records already on file (8b6b8c2f538f1f4a066b2a99bead4a2ddb3fb07a.json subject 'Remove original exFAT driver', df35bdb271325891b3a03c75097541d5b76fbeb6.json subject 'Add exFAT with symlinks support'). (3) ADR 0010 (docs/decisions/0010-drop-out-of-tree-exfat.md) and ADR 0019 (docs/decisions/0019-exfat-symlinks-carried-patch.md, 'amends ADR 0010', explicitly says patch 0031 is 'option (c) from docs/patch-provenance.md §N1') both exist and match the notes' narrative verbatim; 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch confirmed present in board/mister/de10nano/linux-patches/. (4) license_provenance re-verified against the actual commit content: `git show ffbb77e46e:fs/exfat/exfat_core.c` header reads 'Copyright (C) 2012-2013 Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.' under GPL-2.0+ — matches the record exactly. (5) vanilla mainline fs/exfat exists in /mnt/source/linux (v6.18.38) as a from-scratch driver (not Samsung-derived, no native symlink support), consistent with upstream.vanilla_shas=[] and equivalence='superseded-better' being about the vanilla+carried-patch combination, not a pure vanilla absorption — correctly NOT graded dropped-upstream (no single vanilla SHA does what this commit did; getting there required both mainline exfat AND our own carried patch 0031). (6) userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref re-checked: Main_MiSTer file_io.cpp:1592 is the comment 'Handle (possible) symbolic link type in the directory entry' immediately preceding the `de->d_type == DT_LNK || de->d_type == DT_REG` check at line 1593 — citation is accurate (within one line, points at the right block). disposition 'dropped-obsolete' is correct as-is: distinct from a6165424f/ec75e65f (see those records' sonnet-verified notes), this supersession runs through fork-internal reorganization + an ADR-driven carried-patch replacement, not a single clean vanilla SHA, so dropped-upstream would NOT apply here — the existing classification is consistent with this project's taxonomy (dropped-deliberate/dropped-obsolete reserved for policy-mediated or multi-hop supersession chains). source_branch confirmed present and correct: 'MiSTer-v5.13.12'.
